title: Wound dehiscence documentation-only
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

assessment.text: Defines the postoperative surgical-wound population and supports wound dressing, cleansing, wound-healing, and structured postoperative wound-care documentation.
assessment.rationale: Supports postoperative wound and healing documentation for surgical wound dehiscence documentation; no decision is generated.
assessment.evidence_extract: Defines the postoperative surgical-wound population and supports wound dressing, cleansing, wound-healing, and structured postoperative wound-care documentation.

source.title: Surgical site infections: prevention and treatment — Recommendations

follow_up.text: Supports review of suspected infection, cellulitis, microbiology context, structured wound care, and local heat, redness, pain, swelling, discharge, separation, abscess, fever, or systemic signs.
follow_up.rationale: Supports wound separation, discharge, pain, swelling, fever and infection review for surgical wound dehiscence documentation; no decision is generated.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Supports review of suspected infection, cellulitis, microbiology context, structured wound care, and local heat, redness, pain, swelling, discharge, separation, abscess, fever, or systemic signs.

source_limitations: All item-level mappings remain unsupported pending separate clinician review and signed approval.
Operation, wound site and layer, separation length and depth, exposed tissue, fascial integrity, evisceration, systemic status, imaging, treatment, urgent intervention and follow-up remain absent.
No diagnosis, risk conclusion, investigation, treatment, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, escalation or disposition is generated for surgical wound dehiscence documentation.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
